stories.headline: US-Iran Ceasefire on the Brink as Nuclear Talks Stall and Strait of Hormuz Remains Disrupted
stories.summary: President Trump said a ceasefire with Iran expires Wednesday evening and called an extension 'highly unlikely,' while Iran reimposed strict limits on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz after the US seized an Iranian cargo vessel. US officials, including Vice President JD Vance, were travelling to Islamabad, Pakistan, for a second round of peace talks, but Iran signalled it might not attend. The standoff has rattled global oil markets and threatens to escalate into renewed military conflict.
stories.bigWordDef: A formal agreement between fighting sides to stop shooting or attacking each other for a period of time — basically a temporary pause in a war.
stories.thinkQuestion: When two countries disagree about the terms of a peace deal, what kinds of compromises might make an agreement possible, and who should have the power to decide?
stories.videoQuery: Strait of Hormuz explained why it matters global oil

stories.headline: NASA Unveils Fully Assembled Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope Ahead of 2026 Launch
stories.summary: NASA held a media event at Goddard Space Flight Center on April 21 to reveal the completed Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, named after NASA's first chief astronomer. The observatory is expected to have a field of view at least 100 times larger than the Hubble Space Telescope and could measure light from a billion galaxies over its lifetime. It is also designed to directly image planets around nearby stars, a crucial step in the search for life beyond Earth, with launch expected as early as September 2026.
stories.bigWordDef: A special instrument on a telescope that blocks out the blinding light of a star so scientists can see faint planets orbiting around it — like holding up your thumb to block the Sun.
stories.thinkQuestion: The Roman telescope can observe a billion galaxies — how might scientists even begin to make sense of that much data, and what role could artificial intelligence play?
stories.videoQuery: Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope explained NASA

stories.headline: Tilak Varma Scores Maiden IPL Century in 45 Balls, Powers Mumbai Indians to 99-Run Win Over Gujarat Titans
stories.summary: In IPL 2026 Match 30 at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, 23-year-old Tilak Varma smashed an unbeaten 101 off just 45 balls — equalling Sanath Jayasuriya's record for the joint-fastest century in Mumbai Indians history — as MI posted 199/5 and then bowled Gujarat out for a mere 100. Varma, batting at number five when MI were struggling at 46/3, scored 82 of his runs in the final six overs with surgical precision, and also became MI's youngest-ever centurion. The win moved Mumbai Indians up to seventh on the points table with two wins from six matches.
stories.bigWordDef: The ability to recover quickly from a tough situation — like bouncing back after your team is losing badly and turning it into a victory.
stories.thinkQuestion: Tilak Varma scored just 19 runs off his first 22 balls before exploding for 82 in the final 23 — what does this tell us about reading a match situation versus always playing aggressively from ball one?

stories.headline: Karnataka Reels Under Heatwave as IMD Issues Alerts; Bengaluru Touches 36°C
stories.summary: Karnataka is experiencing its peak temperatures of the year, with the India Meteorological Department forecasting that some coastal and Kalyana Karnataka districts could see temperatures close to 45°C. Bengaluru itself is seeing a maximum of around 36°C, about 2–3 degrees above normal, and the IMD warns the broad warming trend will continue even as isolated thunderstorms and hailstorms are expected in some districts this week. Meteorologists note that April has already been hotter than usual and temperatures could climb further in May.
stories.bigWordDef: A prolonged stretch of unusually hot weather that is significantly warmer than normal for a region — it can be dangerous for people, animals, and crops.
stories.thinkQuestion: As cities like Bengaluru get hotter every year, what kinds of urban design changes — from trees to road surfaces to building materials — could help cool them down?
stories.videoQuery: Urban heat island effect India cities explained

stories.headline: India's Defence Minister Rajnath Singh Visits Germany to Strengthen Strategic Defence Partnership
stories.summary: India's Defence Minister Rajnath Singh arrived in Berlin on April 21 for a three-day official visit aimed at deepening the strategic defence partnership between India and Germany. During the trip, the two sides are expected to sign a Defence Industrial Cooperation Roadmap and an agreement on joint training for United Nations peacekeeping operations. High-level visits like this are part of India's broader effort to diversify its defence ties beyond traditional partners.
stories.bigWordDef: Something that involves exactly two parties or countries — like a conversation or deal between India and Germany specifically.
stories.thinkQuestion: Why might India want to build strong defence ties with European countries like Germany in addition to its existing relationships with Russia and the United States?

stories.headline: Lyrid Meteor Shower Peaks Tonight: How to Spot the Annual Light Show from India
stories.summary: The Lyrid meteor shower, one of the oldest known meteor showers, peaks on the night of April 21–22, 2026, produced by debris left behind by Comet Thatcher burning up in Earth's atmosphere. NASA advises skywatchers to look east from around 10 p.m. towards the constellation Lyra, near the bright star Vega. The shower offers an excellent opportunity for amateur astronomers across India, including in Bengaluru, to observe shooting stars — even with the naked eye away from bright city lights.
stories.bigWordDef: A small chunk of rock or dust in space; when it enters Earth's atmosphere and burns up due to friction, we call it a 'meteor' — or a shooting star.
stories.thinkQuestion: Meteor showers happen every year at the same time because Earth passes through the same trail of comet debris — what does this regularity tell us about the predictable nature of our solar system?
stories.videoQuery: Lyrid meteor shower 2026 how to watch explained

videos.title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope Explained
videos.description: An explainer on what the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope will study, how it compares to Hubble, and why it could revolutionise our understanding of dark energy and exoplanets.

videos.title: Lyrid Meteor Shower 2026: How to Watch
videos.description: A guide to watching the annual Lyrid meteor shower, explaining how meteor showers form from comet debris and the best ways to observe them from India.

videos.description: Match highlights of Tilak Varma's stunning maiden IPL hundred in 45 balls for Mumbai Indians against Gujarat Titans, equalling a Sanath Jayasuriya record.

infographic.caption: NASA's Roman Space Telescope: A New Eye on the Universe
infographic.fact: Field of view 100× larger than Hubble · Can observe up to 1 billion galaxies in its lifetime · Fewer than 12 single points of failure vs Hubble's 300+ · Launch expected as early as September 2026 · Named after Nancy Grace Roman, NASA's first chief astronomer
infographic.question: If a telescope can photograph a billion galaxies, what questions about the universe would you most want it to answer — and why?

quiz.q: Why is the Strait of Hormuz so important to the world economy?
options: A) It is the only route for passenger ships between Asia and Europe
B) It is a critical chokepoint for global oil and shipping traffic
C) It is the location of the world's largest underwater internet cables
D) It forms the border between India and Pakistan

quiz.q: What record did Tilak Varma equal with his IPL century against Gujarat Titans?
options: A) The highest individual score in IPL history
B) The most sixes in a single IPL innings
C) The joint-fastest century in Mumbai Indians history, set by Sanath Jayasuriya
D) The fastest century ever scored in T20 cricket worldwide

quiz.q: What is the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope primarily designed to study?
options: A) The surface of Mars and Jupiter's moons
B) Dark energy, exoplanets, and wide-field astrophysics with a view 100× larger than Hubble
C) Asteroids approaching Earth for planetary defence
D) The Sun's solar flares and space weather

quiz.q: The Lyrid meteor shower, peaking on April 21–22, is caused by debris from which object?
options: A) Asteroid Apophis
B) Comet Halley
C) Comet Thatcher
D) Comet Pan-STARRS

quiz.q: Why did India's Defence Minister Rajnath Singh travel to Germany on April 21, 2026?
options: A) To attend a NATO summit as an observer
B) To sign a trade agreement on car manufacturing
C) To strengthen the strategic defence partnership and sign a Defence Industrial Cooperation Roadmap
D) To participate in a United Nations General Assembly session held in Berlin
